title: inga nobilis var. pavoniana
intro: Inga nobilis var. pavoniana, commonly known as the Red Inga, is a tree species in the family Fabaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Inga nobilis var. pavoniana has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including hypertension, fever, and digestive issues, offering potential benefits such as improved cardiovascular health, reduced oxidative stress, and enhanced immune function. The plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to make poultices and ointments for topical application.
